model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current two-layer MLP is still relatively small for flattened CIFAR-10. Increasing the base width raises both hidden layers from 192/384 to 256/512 with minimal code change and should improve representation quality within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With clean CIFAR-10 labels and a fast optimizer, a small amount of label smoothing often improves generalization at essentially zero code or runtime cost, which is attractive under the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, doubling batch size exposes the model to twice as many training examples while keeping the rest of the optimization setup intact. Reverting the label smoothing removes a change that already hurt validation loss, so this tests a cleaner high-throughput variant of the best setup so far.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last improvement came from using more examples per fixed training step. Pushing to batch size 256 should let the run cover essentially the full training set within 256 steps, and scaling the learning rate with the batch size keeps optimization aggressive enough to exploit that extra throughput.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization setup is now much stronger, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. Adding a third hidden block is a minimal architecture change that should increase expressiveness without disturbing the successful high-batch training recipe.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The model is now larger and trained with a high batch size, so decoupled weight decay is more likely to help than Adam's coupled L2 penalty. Switching to AdamW with moderately stronger decay is a compact way to test whether optimization/generalization, rather than raw capacity, is now the limiting factor.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The AdamW detour did not help, so this returns to the stronger baseline optimizer and tests whether the current deeper, high-batch setup is still learning-rate limited. A moderate LR increase is a cheap way to push more progress through the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Larger batches have been the strongest source of gains in this run, while the last change suggests 3e-3 is too aggressive. Moving to batch size 512 while restoring the better 2e-3 rate tests whether even more examples per fixed step improve validation loss without repeating the learning-rate mistake.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The largest improvements so far have consistently come from exposing the model to more examples within the fixed 256-step budget. Moving from batch size 512 to 1024 is the simplest continuation of that trend and should further increase effective dataset coverage without changing the successful optimizer or architecture recipe.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch size has increased from 256 to 1024 while the learning rate stayed fixed, so the current setup is likely under-scaled. A moderate LR increase to 3e-3 is the simplest way to test whether the much larger effective batch can support faster optimization within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The higher learning rate did not help, so this returns to the stronger 2e-3 setting and tests whether capacity is now the bottleneck. A modest width increase keeps the successful depth-3, large-batch recipe intact while giving the network more representational power.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant improvement pattern in this run has been increasing the number of examples processed within the fixed 256-step budget. After a further capacity gain, the simplest next test is to continue that trend with batch size 2048 while leaving the now-strong optimizer and architecture settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 2048, the optimizer is processing far more data per step than in earlier runs while still using the same 2e-3 learning rate. A moderate increase to 3e-3 is a cheap next test for whether the current large-batch regime is now learning-rate limited.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The extra width did not help, which suggests the current expansion pattern may be inefficient rather than capacity-limited. Reverting to the stronger base width and flattening the layer growth to a uniform hidden size tests a simpler, more balanced depth-3 MLP that may optimize better under the large-batch fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The uniform-width variant regressed, so this restores the better expansion pattern and uses the remaining degree of freedom to test slightly lighter regularization. With very large batches and many effective passes through the data, the current setup may now be modestly over-regularized.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The small weight-decay reduction did not beat the best run, so this restores the stronger regularization and continues the clearest positive trend in the search: larger batches have repeatedly improved validation loss under the fixed 256-step budget. Testing batch size 4096 is the highest-signal remaining move.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most reliable improvement signal in the entire run has been increasing batch size under the fixed 256-step budget, and the last jump to 4096 produced another strong gain. With one step left, the highest-upside move is to continue that trend and test batch size 8192 while leaving the rest of the best recipe unchanged.","secondary_modes":[]}
